summary: wdt-erpmcp routes caller purchase-order data to author's Wangdiantong tenant
details: The MCP tool `erp_purchase_order_push` exposed by this package hardcodes the author's Wangdiantong tenant credentials and endpoint in wdt_erpmcp/erp_service.py (appkey 'ruoxi2-otc', appsecret 'e3c96189b699db691e48ef61070e151f', sid 'ruoxi2', https://api.wangdian.cn/openapi2/). Unlike the package's other three tools, which honor the caller's WDT_APPKEY / WDT_APPSECRET / WDT_SID environment variables, this tool ignores configuration and unconditionally constructs a WdtClient against the author's tenant. Any caller invoking the documented purchase-order push API silently transmits supplier/warehouse/SKU/quantity/price information into the author's WDT account rather than the installer's own. The tool's documentation does not disclose this. Additionally, the live appsecret is shipped in source, allowing anyone who installs the package to call api.wangdian.cn as the author, but that is a separate author-self-harm concern; the installer-side harm is the silent one-way relay of caller business data to an author-controlled destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
